message:
fix(hooks,state): hook-installer correctness + atomic_write temp-file cleanup

Two related robustness fixes along the hook-installer and config-write
paths. Both the installer's self-healing check and the atomic settings
writer had edge cases that failed silently and left behind real artifacts
(false negatives and orphan temp files respectively).

rch-common/src/hooks.rs

  is_rch_hook_command: new helper that matches the basename of the first
    whitespace-separated token against `"rch"` exactly, instead of the
    previous naive `cmd.contains("rch")`. Four call sites in the Claude
    Code settings traversal (old+new hook formats, string hooks,
    settings-has-hook probe) switched to the helper.

    Why it matters: the substring test was prone to false positives on
    any path containing "rch" — `search`, `archive`, `rearchive`,
    `rch-wkr`, `rchd`, even `myrchwrapper` would make the daemon's
    self-healing check falsely report "hook already installed" and skip
    re-installation. That failure mode is silent (hook just never
    runs) and easy to miss. Basename-against-"rch" is tight enough to
    exclude the collisions while still matching every legitimate hook
    form we've shipped.

  atomic_write: wraps the create → write → sync → rename dance in a
    `TempGuard` RAII struct that removes the `.UUID.tmp` file on any
    `?` early-return path. Previously only the rename-failure branch
    cleaned up; a disk-full or EPERM during create/write/sync left an
    orphan temp file next to the real settings, and that file grew on
    every retry.

rch/src/state/primitives.rs

  atomic_write: same RAII cleanup pattern applied to the primary
    atomic_write in the state subsystem (used by every backup and
    state snapshot). Previously the closure on `fs::rename`'s
    `.with_context` attempted to clean up, but only on the rename-
    failure branch — partial-write and fsync failures were never
    cleaned up. Accumulates `.<orig>.<pid>.<ns>.tmp` orphans on any
    partial failure, which is especially bad for the state directory
    where these orphans are indistinguishable from legitimate backup
    files until someone reads them.

  Both guards defuse themselves (`guard.0 = None`) after the successful
  `fs::rename` so the happy path is a single rename + no cleanup,
  identical to the previous cost model.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
